  "findings": [
    "Auxiliary heat locked on continuously regardless of thermostat demand, indicating a failed thermostat or stuck sequencer/contactor in the aux heat circuit",
    "Energy waste and potential equipment damage from uncontrolled electric resistance heat operation"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Verify thermostat output signals and wiring integrity at the W2/aux heat terminal",
    "Test aux heat sequencer or contactor for stuck-closed condition and replace if failed",
    "Confirm thermostat heat anticipator or staging logic per OEM manual and recalibrate or replace thermostat as needed"
  ],
  "rationale": "Aux heat locked on while the system is in service points to a control failure in the thermostat or the aux heat switching device (sequencer/contactor). Per OEM service manuals, the thermostat should only call for aux heat during second-stage demand or defrost; continuous operation indicates either a shorted/failed thermostat or a stuck sequencer/contactor that has failed closed.",
  "references": ["mfr manuals"]
}
